other_observers: MOB; Joe Roller and Norm Erthal
specific_location: 16 miles W. of the intersection of Hwy. 160 and Rd. G on Rd. G.
description: This oriole appeared very yellowish overall with an orange wash on the head, nape and breast. Black back with a yellowish-orange rump. Yellowish underparts. Black bill, long and slightly decurved. Black patch on lower face extending to eyes and down over the throat. Long black tail. Black wings with two narrow white wing-bars. Legs grayish.Behaviors: Foraging in an Elm tree.Call: silentPlumage: adult
